sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Establish a one-year independent Cardano dOSPO and Open Maintenance Framework program to manage open source sustainability funds, operate advisory councils, form a 501(c)(3) public charity, fund maintenance retainers, and publish public reporting.
Recipient: Christian Taylor / Open Source Cowboy Consulting as sole Article II.7.5 administrator at withdrawal, with a later possible transition target to a dOSPO legal entity subject to governance.

Deliverables: Two advisory councils chartered with members named, COI policies signed, minutes published, and advisory status confirmed by week 6, dOSPO 501(c)(3) public charity formed with governance documents published and operator agreement executed by week 24, Quarterly outcome reports for all active programs with Mill Law Firm financial review attached, Dependency audit, SBOMs, centrality scores, bus factor assessments, coverage gap analysis, and published retainer selection rubric, Maintenance Fund retainer cohorts selected using rubric scores with on-chain disbursements and published advisory feedback, Public CHAOSS-aligned dashboard tracking funded projects, End-of-term portfolio review with rationale and financial audit attached

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 4,094,000 ADA over 12 months to run four programs: Maintenance Fund, Maintainer Development, CodeForUs bounties, and Ecosystem Activation Reserve.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Christian Taylor / Open Source Cowboy Consulting is designated as the sole administrator at the moment of treasury withdrawal, with final allocation authority during the program term.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Mill Law Firm is designated as the independent financial auditor on a quarterly cadence through month 12.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says no disbursements will be made unilaterally because advisory council feedback will be published before each allocation, while final authority remains with the administrator.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states that governance can replace the administrator or shut down the initiative through an on-chain Info Action and that remaining funds can be returned if governance is unsatisfied.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Maintenance Fund would deploy 2,000,000 ADA over 12 months to fund Cardano open source infrastructure selected by dependency centrality, bus factor, and a published priority formula.
missing: Independent evidence for: Mill Law Firm is designated as the independent financial auditor on a quarterly cadence through month 12.

Independent evidence for: The proposer claims Cardano critical tooling, libraries, governance tools, developer infrastructure, and ecosystem services rely on fragmented coordination, volunteer effort, short-term grants, or individual organizations.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,094,000 ADA over 12 months to run four programs: Maintenance Fund, Maintainer Development, CodeForUs bounties, and Ecosystem Activation Reserve." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Mill Law Firm is designated as the independent financial auditor on a quarterly cadence through month 12." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
